items.title: Buffett's Final Filing: Berkshire Initiates New York Times Stake, Slashes Amazon Position Before CEO Transition
items.description: The latest 13F filing from Berkshire Hathaway reveals some of the final position changes made by Warren Buffett as CEO.

items.title: Can Western Union Beat Q4 Earnings on Consumer Services Strength?
items.description: WU gears up for Q4 as strong Consumer Services growth and lower costs look to counter C2C weakness and fuel an earnings beat.

items.title: The White House Is Threatening Card Issuers Again. Time to Buy Bank Stocks?
items.author: Matthew Benjamin
items.description: The Trump Administration is pressuring credit card issuers to cap interest rates, with White House trade advisor Peter Navarro calling on banks to lower rates. However, legislation to impose rate caps is unlikely due to financial industry opposition. Meanwhile, bank stocks may be attractive investments as the Federal Reserve is expected to cut rates in 2026, which would steepen the yield curve and increase bank profitability.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: As a major card issuer and bank, JPMorgan Chase stands to benefit from a steepening yield curve resulting from Fed rate cuts in 2026, which increases bank profitability through wider lending margins.

items.title: Nasdaq dives to 12-week low to extend losing run, Dow opens higher
items.description: 9.58am: Nasdaq dives at the open It's a mixed start for US stocks, with big tech in the red again. The Nasdaq has opened 1.2% lower to 22,293.85, the lowest since 21 November and on track for what could be a potential sixth weekly reverse in a row. The S&P 500 has dropped...
